artPrompt: A gaunt ashen-robed prophet stands mid-sermon atop overturned crates in a midnight street, arms flung skyward, four faint gold halo-rings of doom orbiting his head, a vast clock tower looming behind against a blood-dark sky. His gesture is feverish, electric, end-times rapture. Guttering candle smoke drifts upward. Bone white and cold gold washes bleeding into black. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Brother Eschaton, Candidate for the End of Days
type: Legendary Acorn Creature — Human Prophet Candidate
text:
When Brother Eschaton enters the battlefield, he becomes the Doomsday Clock: put four doom counters on him. (If a Doomsday Clock already exists, move all its doom counters here instead and apologize for the merger.)
The Doomsday Clock — Once each turn, the first time a player rolls one or more dice or flips one or more coins, put a doom counter on Brother Eschaton. Whenever a Prophecy fails anywhere — his own included — put a doom counter on him.
Prophecy (As this resolves, predict heads or tails out loud and flip a coin.) At the beginning of your upkeep, Brother Eschaton makes a Prophecy. If you predicted correctly, draw a card and remove a doom counter from him. If you didn't, his clock ticks toward midnight (see above).
When Brother Eschaton has twelve or more doom counters on him, it is midnight: each opponent loses life equal to the number of doom counters on him, you gain that much life, then remove all doom counters and say "told you so" out loud.
flavorText: His campaign slogan was "Vote Now, There Won't Be a Next Time." Turnout was, somehow, enormous.

extras.doomsdayClock: hosts the shared doom counter; ticks up once per turn on table gambling and on failed prophecies; midnight at 12
extras.interaction: the whole Prophecy ecosystem already feeds the clock he hosts
